summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the 'Missing Piece' series with a Christmas-week message titled 'Live in Harmony with One Another,' based on Romans 12:16. He acknowledged that December is often a stressful and lonely month, contrasting it with the celebration of Christ's birth. The core message was that biblical peace is not a passive feeling but an intentional discipline we must fight for, especially in tense family situations or conflicts. Pastor Travis explained that being a peacemaker, as Jesus taught in the Beatitudes, means creating peace where it does not yet exist, which often costs us our pride, ego, or desire to be right. Using his musical background, he illustrated harmony as different notes coming together to create something beautiful, a metaphor for how diverse individuals in the church and in relationships should unite. He shared three practical ways to live in harmony: setting our minds on heavenly perspectives, sowing seeds of peace instead of discord, and letting the peace of Christ rule in our hearts. He concluded with a personal testimony about his recovery from a stroke and led the congregation in a moment of surrender, emphasizing that true peace with others starts with peace and surrender to God.
mainPoints: Biblical peace is an intentional discipline we must fight for, not a passive feeling we fall into.
We live in harmony by setting our minds on God's perspective, sowing seeds of peace, and letting Christ's peace rule our hearts.
True peace with others begins with total surrender and peace with God, who is the Prince of Peace.

versesMentioned.text: Love must be sincere. Hate what is evil; cling to what is good. Be devoted to one another in love. Honor one another above yourselves. Never be lacking in zeal, but keep your spiritual fervor, serving the Lord. Be joyful in hope, patient in affliction, faithful in prayer. Share with the Lord’s people who are in need. Practice hospitality. Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse. Rejoice with those who rejoice; mourn with those who mourn. Live in harmony with one another. Do not be proud, but be willing to associate with people of low position. Do not be conceited. Do not repay anyone evil for evil. Be careful to do what is right in the eyes of everyone. If it is possible, as far as it depends on you, live at peace with everyone.
Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.
For to us a child is born, to us a son is given, and the government will be on his shoulders. And he will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.
Follow God’s example, therefore, as dearly loved children.

For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms.
Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things.
Peacemakers who sow in peace reap a harvest of righteousness.

There are six things the Lord hates, seven that are detestable to him: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood, a heart that devises wicked schemes, feet that are quick to rush into evil, a false witness who pours out lies and a person who stirs up conflict in the community.

Do not be deceived: God cannot be mocked. A man reaps what he sows. Whoever sows to please their flesh, from the flesh will reap destruction; whoever sows to please the Spirit, from the Spirit will reap eternal life.
It is to one’s honor to avoid strife, but every fool is quick to quarrel.

Don’t have anything to do with foolish and stupid arguments, because you know they produce quarrels. And the Lord’s servant must not be quarrelsome but must be kind to everyone, able to teach, not resentful.
A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
A person’s wisdom yields patience; it is to one’s glory to overlook an offense.

Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you. And over all these virtues put on love, which binds them all together in perfect unity. Let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, since as members of one body you were called to peace. And be thankful.
And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose.

Monday.scripture: Romans 12:18 (NIV) - 'If it is possible, as far as it depends on you, live at peace with everyone.'
Monday.action: Identify one relationship where tension exists and commit to not escalating any conflict today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to do everything within my power to be a peacemaker today, starting with my own heart.

Tuesday.scripture: Colossians 3:2 (NIV) - 'Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things.'
Tuesday.action: When a frustrating situation arises, pause and ask, 'What is God's bigger perspective here?'
Tuesday.prayer: God, lift my eyes above my immediate circumstances to see my life and conflicts from Your eternal viewpoint.

Wednesday.scripture: James 3:18 (NIV) - 'Peacemakers who sow in peace reap a harvest of righteousness.'
Wednesday.action: Intentionally speak one encouraging or gracious word to someone you find difficult.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, guide my words today. Let me sow seeds of peace, not discord, in every conversation.

Thursday.scripture: Proverbs 15:1 (NIV) - 'A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.'
Thursday.focus: The power of a gentle response.
Thursday.action: Practice responding with a soft tone and calm demeanor, especially if you feel provoked.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, my Prince of Peace, rule my reactions. Let my answers be gentle and diffuse tension.

Friday.scripture: Colossians 3:15 (NIV) - 'Let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts...'
Friday.action: In a moment of quiet, consciously surrender a specific worry or offense to Christ's rule.
Friday.prayer: Christ, I surrender my heart to You. Be the ruler of my emotions, thoughts, and peace today.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 5:9 (NIV) - 'Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on your week. Write down one instance where you chose to make peace and thank God for it.
Saturday.prayer: Father, thank You for calling me Your child. Help me to represent You well by being a maker of peace in my world.
